title: Is LA's standalone-marker inventory POSITION-DIFFERENTIATED? (U+1076B peripheral vs U+2014 interior)
verdict: MARKER_SYSTEM_POSITION_DIFFERENTIATED (mechanical, independently verified incl. all three reconstructed nulls) — Linear A's standalone line-isolated marker inventory is POSITION-DIFFERENTIATED: M1 (U+1076B) brackets document ENDS (peripheral 0.691, E062) while M2 (em-dash U+2014) marks the INTERIOR (peripheral 0.115 vs 0.298 null p_deplete<=0.002; 88.5% interior; heading rate 0.0 vs 0.148 null p_deplete=0.0003 — NEVER a heading). The two markers' positions are significantly COMPLEMENTARY (contrast d=0.576, relabel perm p=0).

title: Is word-INITIAL entropy concentration (prefixing typology, E027 global) CROSS-SITE ROBUST?
verdict: INITIAL_CONCENTRATION_CROSS_SITE_ROBUST (mechanical, independently verified incl. reconstructed null) — Linear A's word-INITIAL sign slot is significantly MORE concentrated (lower entropy) than the word-FINAL slot, GLOBALLY (A=+0.223 bits vs ~0 null, p=0.0005) AND CROSS-SITE: 5-6/10 testable sites individually significant same-direction (A>0), survives leave-one-site-out on the dominant site (HT, ~51% of words; pooled-minus-HT A=+0.332, p=0.0005); no site shows significant reversed (suffixing) concentration. Replicates + generalizes E027's global finding.

title: Is word-length a SITE REGISTER (site-local) beyond document class, or functionally invariant?
verdict: WORDLEN_SITE_REGISTER (mechanical, independently verified incl. reconstructed stratified null) — CONTROLLING for document class, Linear A word-length still carries a significant residual SITE effect (stratified combined KW H=89.6, perm p=0; all 3 testable classes individually significant). Word-length is a SITE-LOCAL scribal REGISTER, not fully determined by document function.

title: Within Tablets, does numeral magnitude (accounting scale) vary by SITE — general register or one-site-driven?
verdict: ACCOUNTING_SCALE_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, independently verified incl. reconstructed null + LOO) — within the Tablet class, Linear A numeral magnitude (accounting scale) differs strongly by SITE (KW H=45.07 vs ~4 null, perm p=0.0002), and the effect is GENERAL: it survives every single-site leave-one-out drop (perm p=0.0002 each), including dropping the dominant site HT (79% of numerals; H=33.74) and the magnitude-outlier Tylissos (H=35.38). Per-site medians span an order of magnitude (1 -> 10). Accounting scale is a site-local register, not one-site-driven.

title: How deep is word-initial concentration? Single prefix slot vs depth-2 (pos1 also concentrated)?
verdict: CONCENTRATION_DEPTH_2 (mechanical, frozen rule met + independently verified) — Linear A word-initial concentration extends to a SECOND slot: position 1 is significantly concentrated beyond the within-word-shuffle null GLOBALLY (A1=0.233 bits, perm p=0.0005), with a monotone decaying gradient A0=0.375 > A1=0.233 > A2=0.128 (all significant) — a prefix-ZONE that decays inward from the word edge, not a single isolated slot. HONEST QUALIFIER: the pos1 signal is GLOBAL + robust to dropping the dominant site (LOO drop-HT A1=0.382), but its per-site replication is PARTIAL and HETEROGENEOUS (2/6 sites individually significant: Zakros, Palaikastro; the dominant HT weak same-direction NS; Phaistos slightly negative). So this is a GLOBAL positive with PARTIAL cross-site support, WEAKER than E064's pos0 (5-6/10 sites).

title: Is the A-prefixed vocabulary MORE cross-site-shared than frequency-matched non-A vocabulary?
verdict: A_MARKED_VOCAB_MORE_SHARED (mechanical, independently verified incl. reconstructed frequency-matched null + power gate) — Linear A word-forms that begin with the A-prefix slot are cross-site-shared at 0.6875 (11/16 informative types) vs 0.3217 (46/143) for FREQUENCY-MATCHED non-A forms; D=+0.371, stratified perm p=0.0072 (power=1.0). The A-marked sub-vocabulary is significantly LESS site-local than general vocabulary.

title: Are entry-INITIAL words longer than entry-internal words (name-then-modifiers), cross-site, beyond the A-prefix?
verdict: ENTRY_INITIAL_WORD_LONGER_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, independently verified incl. reconstructed null + A-exclusion) — Linear A ledger entries open with a LONGER word: entry-initial words average 2.55 signs vs 1.76 for entry-internal words (diff=0.79, ~11 SDs above a within-line word-order shuffle null, perm p=0.0005). The effect is CROSS-SITE (all 3 testable sites significant same direction: HT 1.11, Khania 1.23, Zakros 1.15), survives leave-one-site-out (drop-HT diff=0.54, p=0.0005), and is NOT the A-prefix confound (excluding all A-initial words: diff=0.72, p=0.0005).

title: What do fractions attach to (num->frac metrological vs word->frac)? Cross-site template or site-local?
verdict: FRAC_ATTACHMENT_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, independently verified incl. nulls + site contrast) — the token-class a Linear A fraction ATTACHES TO (what immediately precedes it) is a SITE convention, not a uniform cross-site metrological template. Haghia Triada attaches fractions to NUMERALS at R_num=0.479 (metrological 'N and a fraction', enriched vs null 0.163) while Khania attaches them overwhelmingly to WORDS (R_num only 0.055 vs word ~0.67); the two sites differ significantly (site-contrast perm p=0.0002, |diff|=0.425). num->frac is enriched globally (0.387 vs 0.130) and even weakly at Khania (0.055 vs 0.021), but the DEGREE/dominant attachment is site-local.

title: Does the LIBATION (stone-vessel) corpus have a cross-site formula beyond frequency, unlike site-local admin vocab?
verdict: LIBATION_FORMULA_SITE_LOCAL_LIKE_ADMIN (mechanical, independently verified) — at the INDIVIDUAL-WORD-FORM level, the libation (stone-vessel) corpus does NOT recur cross-site beyond word-form frequency + site-size: S_obs=20 sits BELOW its reassignment null (ratio 0.6-0.8, perm p≈1). The famous recurring forms (A-TA-I-*301-WA-JA 5 sites, SI-RU-TE 5, I-PI-NA-MA 5, JA-SA-SA-RA-ME 4, U-NA-KA-NA-SI 3) appear at many sites BECAUSE THEY ARE FREQUENT, not beyond chance. The administrative corpus behaves identically (S_obs=23 vs null ~92-97, ratio 0.24). NO genre-dependent lexicon-sharing contrast at the form-spread level.

title: Does the LIBATION formula recur as an ORDERED sequence (shared canonical order) across sites, beyond a within-inscription order-shuffle null? (E071 successor: vocabulary was frequency-explained; is the ORDER real?)
verdict: LIBATION_FORMULA_ORDERED_SEQUENCE_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, independently verified) — the libation (stone-vessel) word-forms appear in a PERFECTLY CONSISTENT canonical relative ORDER that is SHARED ACROSS SITES: all 13 testable form-pairs and all 10 cross-site pairs (spanning up to 4 sites) have order-consistency 1.000, decisively beyond a within-inscription word-order shuffle null (C_glob 1.0 vs 0.740, C_cross 1.0 vs 0.737, A_cross 10 vs ~3.7-4.3; all perm p=0.0005). The formula is REAL AS AN ORDERED SEQUENCE.

title: GENRE CONTRAST — is a rigid canonical within-inscription word-ORDER a general LA feature, or LIBATION-specific? (control for E072: apply the order-consistency machinery to the ADMIN corpus)
verdict: ADMIN_ORDER_WEAKER_THAN_LIBATION (mechanical, independently verified) — administrative (Tablet/Nodule/Roundel) texts have a WEAK-but-real within-inscription word-order preference (C_glob=0.839 vs shuffle null 0.745, perm p=0.0015; NOT bag-of-words), but it is significantly LESS rigid than the libation formula's perfectly-consistent canonical order (admin bootstrap upper 0.887 << libation 1.000, delta=0.161). The PERFECTLY RIGID canonical cross-site order is LIBATION-SPECIFIC (a liturgical property), NOT a general Linear A feature. Admin's CROSS-SITE order is structurally UNDERPOWERED (only 3 cross-site co-occurring pairs vs libation's 10) — the predicted downstream consequence of E071 (admin vocabulary is site-local, so forms rarely co-occur across sites).

title: CROSS-GENRE LEXICON PARTITION vs the GENRE-SITE CONFOUND — is the striking libation/admin lexicon partition a GENRE effect, or E071's SITE-locality re-expressed? (guilty-until-proven-innocent)
verdict: GENRE_SITE_CONFOUNDED_UNDERDETERMINED (mechanical, independently verified) — the libation/admin lexicon partition is STRIKING globally (20 shared forms vs frequency-preserving null ~69, p_below~0, Jaccard 0.024) BUT it COLLAPSES under site control (site-stratified null 21.5 ~ observed 20, p_below_sitestrat=0.26): once each form's find-site is held fixed and only genre may vary within a site, NO residual genre-lexicon effect is detectable. Genre and find-site are near-perfectly confounded (83.4% of tokens genre-determined by a single-genre site; only Palaikastro has both genres >=10 tokens). The frozen rule returns UNDERDETERMINED (n_both_sites<2): the partition is REAL but CANNOT be attributed to genre beyond E071's established site-locality of vocabulary.

title: HARDEN the E072 libation-order cross-site positive — does the canonical order survive leave-one-SITE-out and leave-the-HUB-inscription-out, or is its cross-site breadth concentration-driven? (invariant #3 + Art. VIII effective-n)
verdict: LIBATION_ORDER_CROSS_SITE_QUALIFIED (mechanical, independently verified) — E072 is a REAL but NARROW cross-site positive. The canonical libation order signal is GENUINE and robust (survives EVERY leave-one-site-out incl. the hub site Iouktas at the power floor, and survives dropping the hub inscription IOZa2 alone; C_cross stays 1.0 in every powered leave-out — it NEVER collapses toward the null). BUT its cross-site BREADTH is inscription-concentrated: 10 raw cross-site pairs but effective-n only 8 inscriptions / 6 sites, with the hub IOZa2 an endpoint of all 10 and ~3 inscriptions carrying most of the weight (dropping the top-2 carriers reduces the test to a powerless n_cross=3). E072 should be read as 'a canonical order shared across a SMALL set of libation inscriptions spanning several sites', NOT a broad corpus-wide formula. APPEND-ONLY QUALIFICATION of E072 (Art. XVII), not a superseding correction — E072's verdict stands.

title: FIRST SPATIAL epoch (new modality: SigLA per-glyph bounding boxes) — is normalized glyph SIZE a shared cross-site graphic convention (like the E036 frequency fingerprint) or site-local (like E017/E020 allograph shape)?
verdict: GLYPH_SIZE_SHARED_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, independently verified) — a sign's normalized glyph SIZE (within-document z-scored log bbox-area) is a SHARED cross-site graphic property: all 5 testable site-pairs among the 5 big sites show large positive correlation of per-sign size profiles (r 0.70-0.85, median 0.79; perm_p ≤ 0.0016 vs a sign-label-shuffle null over 5000 draws). Glyph size follows the SHARED sign-frequency-fingerprint pattern (E036), NOT the site-local allograph-shape pattern (E017/E020).

title: SPATIAL — does glyph SIZE carry POSITIONAL information (document-initial prominence / heading marker), or is it purely a per-sign property? (successor to E076)
verdict: SPATIAL_SIZE_POSITION_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, independently verified) — glyph SIZE does NOT mark document-initial prominence CROSS-SITE: global D_init=-0.057 (initial glyph slightly SMALLER, not larger), one-sided initial-larger p=0.814 (not significant). Only 1 of 5 sites (Haghia Triada, D_init=+0.148, p=0.048 borderline) trends initial-larger; the other four trend initial-smaller. The positional size effect is at most a weak, non-robust LOCAL habit (HT), not a shared cross-site spatial marker. Glyph size carries no cross-site positional/heading information — it is primarily a per-SIGN property (E076).

title: SPATIAL — glyph-size ECONOMY OF EFFORT: are frequent signs drawn SMALLER (a spatial Zipf's-law-of-abbreviation analog), controlling for sign class, cross-site?
verdict: GLYPH_SIZE_FREQUENCY_ECONOMY_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, independently verified) — more FREQUENT signs are drawn systematically SMALLER: r_all=-0.351 over 89 signs (frequency-shuffle perm p=0.0004), a spatial ECONOMY-OF-EFFORT / Zipf's-law-of-abbreviation analog in the glyph modality. It SURVIVES the sign-class control (within the homogeneous AB syllabary r_AB=-0.317, perm p=0.005; within class A r_A=-0.620), and the AB/A classes have ~equal mean size (0.112 vs 0.081) — so it is NOT the logogram/syllabogram size-complexity confound. Cross-site: significant negative in the 2 well-powered sites (Haghia Triada r=-0.376 p=0.0007; Khania r=-0.480 p=0.0033); the 3 lower-n sites trend negative (Zakros -0.18, Phaistos -0.24) or are too small (Knossos n=8).

title: SPATIAL — inter-glyph SPACING regularity: is within-line horizontal spacing a deliberate ruled/monospaced-like layout (pitch more uniform than a random gap-allocation), cross-site?
verdict: SPACING_REGULAR_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, independently verified) — inter-glyph horizontal spacing within a line is REGULAR (ruled / monospaced-like), far beyond a random gap-allocation null: global median doc pitch-CV = 0.329 over 246 docs vs random-composition null-median 0.743 (perm p=0.0 over 2000 draws; ratio 0.44). Observed center-to-center pitches are roughly half as variable as a random allocation of the same row spans. Cross-site: significant regular spacing in 3/3 testable sites (>=15 usable docs) — Haghia Triada (S=0.354, p=0.0), Khania (S=0.268, p=0.0), Zakros (S=0.346, p=0.0), each far below its site-specific null (~0.69-0.76). Khania is the most regular. The observed ~0.33 is regular-but-hand-inscribed (not machine-perfect ~0.20) — an implicit columnar/ruled grid, not a typeface.

title: SPATIAL — VERTICAL line spacing regularity: is row-to-row pitch a deliberate ruled/regular layout (ruled horizontal lines / page grid), beyond a clustering-conditional random row-allocation null, cross-site? (orthogonal partner to E079)
verdict: LINE_SPACING_REGULAR_CROSS_SITE (mechanical rule; coordinator-adjudicated null; independently verified) — vertical row-to-row spacing (line pitch) is a deliberate ruled/regular layout, far more uniform than a clustering-conditional random row-allocation null: global median doc line-pitch-CV = 0.118 over 197 docs vs conditional null-median 0.354 (perm p=0.0 over 2000 draws; conservative ratio 0.33; the naive unconditional null gives 0.64, ratio 0.17, also perm p=0). Cross-site: significant regular line spacing in 3/3 testable sites (>=15 usable docs) — Haghia Triada (S=0.121, null 0.363, p=0.0), Khania (S=0.118, null 0.313, p=0.0), Zakros (S=0.108, null 0.351, p=0.0). Line spacing (CV ~0.11) is even MORE regular than E079's within-line glyph spacing (CV ~0.33) — lines are a coarser, more deliberate ruling. WITH E079 this establishes a full 2D RULED GRID (regular in both axes), cross-site.

title: SPATIAL — LEFT-MARGIN anchoring: are rows left-justified (left edge aligned, right edge ragged), beyond an edge-exchangeability swap null, cross-site? (third 2D-layout axis: ANCHORING, orthogonal to E079/E080 SPACING)
verdict: LEFT_MARGIN_ANCHORED_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, independently verified) — rows are LEFT-ANCHORED (left edge aligned, right edge ragged = left-justified layout) GLOBALLY (S_obs=+0.459, edge-swap null ~ -0.026, perm p=2e-4; median normalized left-edge spread 0.66 vs right-edge spread 1.25; 65.5% of docs left-tighter-than-right), but the effect is SITE-LOCAL to Haghia Triada: HT strongly left-anchored (S=0.518, perm p=2e-4, n=124); Khania NOT (S=-0.03, perm p=0.55 — edges exchangeable, no left-justification); Zakros positive but UNDERPOWERED (S=0.44, perm p=0.114, n=21, stable at ~0.10 to 20k draws). Only 1/3 testable sites significant -> SITE_LOCAL. CONTRAST WITH E079/E080: the 2D grid is evenly SPACED in both axes CROSS-SITE (E079 horizontal 3/3, E080 vertical 3/3), but left-JUSTIFICATION/anchoring is a Haghia Triada register, NOT a cross-site universal. So the 'shared 2D ruled grid' is qualified: spacing conventions are shared; margin justification is site-local.

title: HARDENING — is the 2D-ruled-grid (E079 h-spacing + E080 v-line-spacing) ROBUST to the row-detection method, or an artifact of the shared 0.6x-median-glyph-height clustering threshold? (invariant #3, analog of E075 hardening E072)
verdict: MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE (mechanical, coordinator-confirmed) — the epoch does NOT certify the 2D-grid's robustness because its own false-positive positive-control cannot be calibrated: the pitch-CV metric conflates ruled-grid spacing with fixed-glyph-width regularization, so glyph-level random layouts are flagged as regular (FP 0.20 worker / up to 1.00 coordinator). The RAW threshold-sweep is nonetheless robust + coordinator-verified (S_h 0.33-0.38, S_v 0.11-0.12 at all thresholds << nulls 0.76/0.36; 3/3 sites) — but robust raw statistics without a passing PC earn NO certification. E079/E080 are UNAFFECTED (they passed their own PITCH-LEVEL PCs). E082 surfaces an honest CAVEAT on E079: part of the horizontal-spacing regularity may reflect uniform glyph widths (E076) rather than purely deliberate even spacing.
artifact_paths: epochs/EPOCH-082/prereg.md; epochs/EPOCH-082/plan_hash.txt; epochs/EPOCH-082/machinery.py; epochs/EPOCH-082/result.json; epochs/EPOCH-082/run_output.json; epochs/EPOCH-082/COORDINATOR_NOTE.md

title: Is E079's horizontal pitch-regularity DELIBERATE even spacing or a glyph-WIDTH artifact? (width-preserving random-gap null; QUALIFIES E079). Successor of the E082 hardening caveat.
verdict: HORIZONTAL_REGULARITY_WIDTH_DRIVEN (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — E079's horizontal center-to-center pitch-regularity is driven by uniform glyph WIDTH (E076), NOT by deliberate even GAP spacing. Against a WIDTH-PRESERVING random-gap null (keep each glyph's actual width + row span, randomize only the gaps), the observed pitch-CV is NOT below the null (global 0.338 vs 0.271, perm_p=1.0, ratio 1.25 — observed is at/above the null); consistent across all 3 sites (HT/Khania/Zakros, all perm_p=1.0, deliberate=false). The inter-glyph GAPS are not deliberately regular. This QUALIFIES E079 (Art. XVII): E079's measurement stands (pitch is more regular than a random-COMPOSITION null) but its INTERPRETATION as 'ruled/monospaced-like deliberate spacing' is refuted — the horizontal grid is a glyph-size-uniformity effect. E082 flagged this (failed PC); E083 certifies it (passing PC).
artifact_paths: epochs/EPOCH-083/prereg.md; epochs/EPOCH-083/plan_hash.txt; epochs/EPOCH-083/machinery.py; epochs/EPOCH-083/result.json; epochs/EPOCH-083/run_output.json; epochs/EPOCH-083/COORDINATOR_NOTE.md; reports/EPOCH083_REPORT.md

title: Is E080's VERTICAL line-spacing regularity DELIBERATE or a line-HEIGHT artifact? (height-preserving random-gap null; CONFIRMS/hardens E080). Symmetric partner of E083.
verdict: LINE_SPACING_DELIBERATE_CROSS_SITE (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — E080's vertical line-spacing regularity is DELIBERATE, not a row-height artifact. Against a HEIGHT-PRESERVING random-gap null (keep each row's height + column span, randomize only the inter-line gaps), the observed line-pitch-CV is significantly BELOW the null (global 0.111 vs 0.161, perm_p=0.002, ratio 0.69), significant-deliberate in 2/3 testable sites (HT p=0.002, Zakros p=0.032; Khania right-direction but underpowered p=0.18). The inter-line GAPS ARE deliberately regular. This CONFIRMS + HARDENS E080 (Art. XVII): E080's regularity is not just 'above a random-row-allocation null' but genuinely DELIBERATE beyond row height. The OPPOSITE of E083's horizontal WIDTH-DRIVEN finding: the 2D 'grid' is deliberately-ruled LINES (vertical) whose within-line glyph regularity is a byproduct of uniform glyph SIZE (horizontal, E083).
artifact_paths: epochs/EPOCH-084/prereg.md; epochs/EPOCH-084/plan_hash.txt; epochs/EPOCH-084/machinery.py; epochs/EPOCH-084/result.json; epochs/EPOCH-084/run_output.json; epochs/EPOCH-084/COORDINATOR_NOTE.md; reports/EPOCH084_REPORT.md

title: Do scribes mark the document HEADING (top spatial row) with LARGER glyphs than the body — a size-based visual hierarchy — and is it cross-site or a site register? DISTINCT axis: glyph SIZE x document REGION (E077 was size x word-initial, E078 size x frequency, E079-084 spacing/pitch).
verdict: HEADING_SIZE_HIERARCHY_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — a heading-size hierarchy (top/first spatial row drawn with LARGER glyphs than the body) is a HAGHIA TRIADA register, NOT a cross-site Linear A convention. Per-doc log area-ratio R = log(median first-row glyph AREA / median rest AREA); global median S_obs=+0.048 is NOT significant vs a random-row-as-heading null (perm_p=0.126, n=215 docs). Only Haghia Triada shows it: HT S_obs=+0.092, perm_p=0.020 (SIGNIFICANT, n=128). Khania is REVERSED (S_obs=-0.141, perm_p=0.977 — top row SMALLER, n=29) and Zakros is null (S_obs=-0.017, perm_p=0.539, n=20). 1/3 testable sites significant -> SITE_LOCAL. This MIRRORS E081's HT-only left-margin justification: Haghia Triada carries document-layout register conventions (left-justification E081 + heading-size salience E085) that Khania and Zakros do NOT share, while glyph SPACING (E079/E080/E084) and glyph SIZE conventions (E076/E078) ARE cross-site. L2, opaque IDs, geometry only.
artifact_paths: epochs/EPOCH-085/prereg.md; epochs/EPOCH-085/plan_hash.txt; epochs/EPOCH-085/machinery.py; epochs/EPOCH-085/result.json; epochs/EPOCH-085/run_output.json; reports/EPOCH085_REPORT.md

title: Does the TOP (first) spatial row carry MORE glyphs than lower rows (a full/header line), cross-site or site-local — and is it WIDTH/geometry-driven or a deliberately PACKED header? DISTINCT axis: row GLYPH-COUNT/density by row position (E085 was row SIZE; E079-084 spacing/pitch).
verdict: HEADER_ROW_LONGER_SITE_LOCAL (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — the TOP (first) spatial row carries MORE glyphs than lower rows (a longer 'header line'), but this is a HAGHIA TRIADA register, NOT a cross-site convention, AND it is WIDTH/geometry-driven (a full-width top line), NOT a deliberately packed header. Per-doc log COUNT-ratio R = log(count(first row)/mean count(rest)); global median S_obs=+0.065 is significant vs a random-row-as-heading null (perm_p=0.010, n=240) but concentrates in HT: HT S_obs=+0.138 perm_p=0.004 (SIG, n=140); Khania S_obs=0.0 perm_p=0.909 (null, n=36); Zakros S_obs=+0.083 perm_p=0.119 (marginal, n=23). 1/3 testable sites significant -> SITE_LOCAL. The SECONDARY span-normalized DENSITY metric is NOT significant (global -0.047 perm_p=0.108; HT -0.126 perm_p=0.636 — top row is if anything LESS dense) -> the top row is longer because it is physically WIDER (spans the full document width), NOT because glyphs are packed tighter — WIDTH-driven, exactly parallel to E083's horizontal WIDTH-DRIVEN finding. The SITE-LOCAL pattern (HT yes / Khania no) shows this is a HT layout habit, NOT universal tablet geometry (which would appear at every site). This is a THIRD convergent HT document-LAYOUT register alongside E081 (left-margin justification) and E085 (heading-size salience). L2, opaque IDs, counts/positions only.
artifact_paths: epochs/EPOCH-086/prereg.md; epochs/EPOCH-086/plan_hash.txt; epochs/EPOCH-086/machinery.py; epochs/EPOCH-086/result.json; epochs/EPOCH-086/run_output.json; reports/EPOCH086_REPORT.md

title: Do HT's three document-layout registers (justification E081, heading-size E085, header-length E086) CO-OCCUR in the same docs (a coherent formal-layout TEMPLATE) or are they statistically INDEPENDENT habits? Synthesis test of the site-local register cluster.
verdict: LAYOUT_REGISTERS_DEPENDENT_HT (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — Haghia Triada's three document-layout registers (left-justification E081, heading-size E085, header-length E086) are statistically DEPENDENT (jointly NON-independent), BUT via a MIXED-sign structure, NOT a coherent all-positive 'formal template'. Coherence C = mean|pairwise Spearman| = 0.160 exceeds a per-column-independence null (perm_p=0.007, n=128 HT docs), and survives size control (residualize on log n_glyphs: C 0.156, perm_p 0.011). The signed pairwise correlations are MIXED: justif~h_size -0.143, justif~h_len +0.149, h_size~h_len -0.189. The DOMINANT structure is a heading-size <-> header-length TRADEOFF (r=-0.189): docs whose top row has LARGER glyphs have SHORTER top rows (fewer glyphs) -- a title-vs-full-line dichotomy, the OPPOSITE of an all-positive template where 'formal' docs would show large AND long headers. So the E081/E085/E086 HT registers are not random independent habits, but they also do NOT bundle into one 'formality' axis: the main relationship is a top-row size<->length tradeoff. L2, opaque IDs, geometry only, within-HT.

title: Is E087's heading-size<->header-length TRADEOFF a fixed first-row INK BUDGET (total header glyph-area ~ constant) or a soft tendency? Mechanism test on the HT header row.
verdict: HEADER_INK_NOT_BUDGETED (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — E087's heading-size <-> header-length tradeoff (Spearman -0.189) is a SOFT design tendency, NOT the byproduct of a fixed first-row INK BUDGET. The CV of first-row total ink (sum of glyph areas) across 140 HT docs is 0.639, only marginally below the independence null (random median-area * random count) median 0.714 (ratio 0.896), and NOT significantly so (perm_p=0.145). A fixed header ink budget is REJECTED at alpha=0.05. Total header ink varies freely (CV ~0.64) across documents; scribes do not allocate a constant glyph-area budget to the header row. So the size<->length tradeoff is a real but SOFT anti-correlation (bigger heading glyphs tend to be fewer), not a hard area constraint. L2, opaque IDs, areas/counts only, within-HT.

title: Is the header row's horizontal WRITING WIDTH (x-extent) constrained to a roughly fixed width, explaining E087's size<->count tradeoff (after E088 rejected a fixed INK budget)? Mechanism test on the HT header row.
verdict: HEADER_WIDTH_CONSTRAINED (mechanical, PC-passed, coordinator-verified) — the header (first) row's horizontal WRITING EXTENT is significantly MORE constant than random glyph assembly, i.e. it fits a roughly fixed writing WIDTH; this is the likely mechanism for E087's heading-size <-> header-length tradeoff (which E088 showed is NOT a fixed ink/area budget). The CV of first-row x-extent (max glyph right - min glyph left) across 140 HT docs is 0.407, significantly BELOW an assembly null that rebuilds each doc's extent from pooled first-row glyph widths + inter-glyph gaps at the doc's actual count (null median 0.545, ratio 0.747, perm_p=0.0005). The header row spans ~84% of the document x-extent (near-full-width, consistent with E086). So within a bounded horizontal writing area, bigger first-row glyphs mechanically leave room for fewer -> the size<->count tradeoff. HONEST CAVEAT: a constrained extent may reflect the TABLET dimension OR a scribal writing-area convention (a successor, E090, separates them by support type); the finding is 'the writing extent is constrained', not 'scribes deliberately targeted a width'. L2, opaque IDs, positions/widths only, within-HT.

title: Is E089's header WIDTH-constraint HEADER-SPECIFIC or a DOC-WIDE property (all rows fit a bounded writing width)? Confound-check qualifying E089.
verdict: WIDTH_CONSTRAINT_DOC_WIDE (mechanical verdict; PC adjudicated -> conclusion banked on coordinator reconstruction, Art. XVII) -- E089's header width-constraint is NOT header-specific; it is a DOC-WIDE property. Header rows and body rows are equally width-constrained: ratio_header (extent-CV / assembly-null) = 0.747, ratio_body = 0.755-0.757 -- both ~25% more width-regular than random glyph assembly, to the SAME degree (D_obs = -0.008 to -0.010, ~0). A proper doc-level clustered bootstrap 95% CI [-0.102,+0.088] INCLUDES 0. So ALL rows of an HT document fit the same bounded writing width (the writing area), and E089's 'header width constraint' is really a whole-document property, NOT a header design feature. This QUALIFIES E089 (Art. XVII): E089's HEADER_WIDTH_CONSTRAINED measurement stands, but its scope is refined from 'the header is width-constrained' to 'the document writing width is bounded and all rows including the header fit it'. The E087 heading size<->count tradeoff is thus a consequence of the doc-wide bounded writing width applied to the (near-full-width) header row, not a header-specific budget or convention. L2, opaque IDs, positions/widths only, within-HT.

title: Blinded Linear B Turing reaction-diffusion calibration: does graph RD (Schnakenberg/GM, mechanically-verified Turing instability) recover vowel/consonant/role classes from blinded LB co-occurrence graphs, above negative controls?
verdict: TURING_LINEAR_B_RECOVERY_NULL (mechanical) -- a graph reaction-diffusion (Turing) system on blinded Linear B sign graphs does NOT recover phonetic class structure via its diffusion-driven instability above negative controls. Turing conditions verified on Schnakenberg + Gierer-Meinhardt across all 4 graph views; PC fires 5/5. But: best blinded-LB role recovery (FORMULA/GM macro-F1 0.765) is MATCHED/BEATEN by equal-diffusion with the Turing mechanism OFF (0.779, same MULTILAYER machinery) and by degree-preserving rewiring (p95 0.739); vowel recovery is weak (best 0.453, SUBSTITUTION) and consonant ~chance (best 0.195, 8-class). Condition 4 largely FAILS on real LB: |corr(pattern,degree)| = 0.5-0.7 across views -- the emergent pattern tracks node degree, not a morphogenetic class field. So whatever structure is recoverable is GENERIC graph structure, not a diffusion-driven Turing mechanism. L2, opaque sign IDs, no licence earned. Directly consistent with the EPOCH-016 SBI prior (generic clustering ties/beats fancy methods on this corpus).

title: Turing-specificity: is any blinded-LB class recovery due to diffusion-driven morphogenesis vs generic graph clustering (spectral/eigenmap-kmeans/eigenmap-GMM/Louvain/linear-diffusion/equal-diffusion/reaction-only), best-of-view?
verdict: GENERIC_GRAPH_CLUSTERING (TURING_NOT_NEEDED) (mechanical) -- graph reaction-diffusion (Turing) morphogenesis is NOT specifically required on blinded Linear B: a generic graph-clustering baseline matches or beats Turing on EVERY channel. role: linear-diffusion 0.798 > Turing 0.675 (eigenmap-kmeans 0.636 ~ Turing); vowel: Louvain 0.491 and eigenmap-kmeans 0.490 > Turing 0.453; consonant: linear-diffusion 0.200 ~ Turing 0.194 (all ~chance, 8-class). All 6 methods pass the planted PC 5/5, so the baselines are fair. The diffusion-driven Turing instability confers no recovery advantage over generic spectral/community/heat-kernel clustering. L2, opaque IDs, LA not touched, no licence. Directly consistent with EPOCH-016 (SBI: raw-cosine spectral ties/beats fancy methods) and EPOCH-091 (equal-diffusion ties full Turing). Together E091+E092 REFUTE the F11 central hypothesis (that a Turing mechanism recovers linguistic structure better than generic clustering).

title: LB->LA degradation surface: 108-cell (token_budget x min_count x seed) factorial degrading LB toward LA data scale; is LA above/below the detection threshold for the generic-best graph method, and is data volume the binding constraint?
verdict: LINEAR_A_ABOVE_THRESHOLD (mechanical) -- at Linear A's data scale (~4245 tokens, nearest cell 4000) the generic-best graph method's blinded-LB vowel-class recovery beats BOTH its permutation floor AND a frequency-only baseline (and a degree-only baseline) in 100% of subsamples, with a margin over frequency (+0.13) that is STABLE across the full 2000->43868-token range. So LA's DATA VOLUME is sufficient to detect the weak, frequency/degree-independent phonetic-class co-occurrence signal that exists in blinded LB -- data scarcity is NOT the binding constraint for this signal. CRITICAL SCOPE: the signal is WEAK in absolute terms (vowel macro-F1 ~0.44, ceiling ~0.49) and this is BLINDED LINEAR B; the verdict licenses NO LA sound values, NO vowel readings, NO transfer (L2, opaque IDs, brief S11). It REFINES the underdetermination story: the binding constraint on LA is NOT data volume but (a) the intrinsic weakness of the phonetic-class signal in sign co-occurrence and (b) the absence of a licensed LB->LA phonetic transfer (established in prior campaigns). The trivial role/degree channel is itself data-limited (detected 0% under the frequency guard; role correlates with frequency).

title: Sequence/segmentation morphogenesis: does an RD activator-domain boundary cue recover blinded-LB word boundaries vs transition-surprisal / branching-entropy / random, held-out?
verdict: SEGMENTATION_MORPHOGENESIS_NULL (mechanical) -- an RD/morphogenesis boundary cue (activator-domain differences |u(a)-u(b)| over the G_POSITION sign-type graph) carries NO word-boundary signal on blinded LB: morphogenesis boundary-F1 0.310 ~ random 0.304, while a generic transition-surprisal cue recovers boundaries well (0.650) and branching entropy partially (0.369). The result is architectural: a STATIC morphogenetic field is a type-level quantity and cannot encode a SEQUENTIAL/transition property like a word boundary -- confirmed on the PC (surprisal 0.87 but morphogenesis only 0.363 even where boundaries are trivially detectable). L2, opaque IDs, LA not touched, no licence. Consistent with E091/E092 (morphogenesis ties/loses to generic methods). Byproduct (generic, not morphogenesis): transition surprisal is a strong unsupervised LB boundary cue (0.650 boundary-precision-at-true-rate).

title: Geographic/scribal morphogenesis: does an RD document-community process recover LA site structure vs spectral/Louvain/length-confound/random, balanced across sites?
verdict: GEOGRAPHIC_MORPHOGENESIS_NULL (mechanical) -- morphogenesis does not recover LA site/regional structure from blinded document sign-content beyond the confound floor (morphogenesis ARI 0.019 vs length-only 0.059, random 0.000). BROADER FINDING: once site imbalance is controlled (balanced to 80 docs/site), NO method recovers site communities from document sign-content -- spectral 0.011, Louvain 0.043, all ~chance; the length-only confound (0.059) is nominally the 'best', i.e. what little separates balanced sites is document LENGTH, not sign content. LA sites share one syllabary, so blinded doc-level sign-content does not cluster by site. Consistent with the campaign's E012 (doc->site NO_POWER/confounded); orthogonal to the SPECIFIC cross-site signals that ARE real (A-prefix E023, site registers E065) which are feature-level, not whole-document-community. L2, site = structural metadata, opaque signs, no phonetic values, no licence, no dialect claim.

title: Error-correcting-code + belief-propagation decoding: masked-sign reconstruction via BP over a sign factor graph (bidirectional + skip-2 redundancy) vs unigram/local/random; blinded LB -> degraded-to-LA-scale -> frozen LA.
verdict: ECC_BP_SUPPORTED on the LB CALIBRATION, but LA_TRANSFER_NEGATIVE (the LA objective is a NULL) -- headline is the transfer failure (Art. XV / brief S15: LB success is not LA evidence). On blinded LB, BP message-passing clearly beats local context and unigram (bp_loopy 0.307 / bp_bidir 0.279 vs best-local 0.158 vs unigram 0.044 vs random 0.003), and the advantage survives degrading LB to LA token scale (bp_bidir 0.214 vs local 0.157). BUT on REAL Linear A the advantage REVERSES: forward-bigram is the best method (0.121), bp_bidir underperforms it (0.087), and loopy BP is ACTIVELY HARMFUL (0.038, BELOW unigram 0.058) -- LA's longer (mean 7.88 vs LB 3.23), sparser words break the higher-order/bidirectional estimates, so the 'code redundancy' machinery does not transfer. NET FOR LA: masked-sign prediction is best served by a plain forward-bigram at modest accuracy (top-1 0.121); belief propagation adds NOTHING and its higher-order form hurts. L2 (sign identity, no phonetic values, no translations). A calibration-positive / transfer-negative -- the campaign's signature pattern (cf. E016 SBI, F11 morphogenesis).

title: Spin-glass / Potts energy-landscape identifiability: signed-coupling Potts over sign classes, simulated annealing, ground-state degeneracy + anchor-injection phase-transition sweep; blinded LB (vowel) + LA.
verdict: POTTS_NULL (mechanical) -- the context-similarity Potts energy landscape does NOT make the linguistic (vowel) class system identifiable, and anchor injection produces NO identifiability transition. On blinded LB the landscape has a NEAR-UNIQUE minimum (inter-run overlap 0.997, using all 5/5 states -- NOT a trivial single-cluster collapse) but that minimum is ORTHOGONAL to vowel classes: free-spin vowel recovery stays near chance (0.29 at f=0 -> 0.38 at f=0.5 anchors; 1/q=0.20), and injecting TRUE-vowel anchors DROPS inter-run overlap 1.00 -> 0.55 while barely moving recovery -- i.e. the truth-anchors CONFLICT with the couplings (added frustration), proving the evidence is orthogonal to the target. LA behaves the same (overlap 0.917, 5/5 states). So this is NOT the classic glassy/many-minima picture; it is 'landscape determined but uninformative about the linguistic classes'. A mechanism-level confirmation that the available structural evidence (context similarity) does not render LA (or blinded LB) class assignment identifiable -- consistent with E091/E092 (context graph orthogonal to vowel) and the anchor-lattice underdetermination. NO value assigned from any minimum. L2, opaque signs, no phonetic values.

title: Causal source separation + confound disentanglement: ICA/NMF/PCA source separation + leave-one-frequency-band-out + deconfounding invariance test on the blinded-LB vowel signal vs the frequency nuisance.
verdict: CAUSAL_INVARIANT_STRUCTURE_SUPPORTED on blinded LINEAR B (LB-calibration positive; NOT an LA claim). The weak vowel-class signal survives BOTH nuisance interventions: leave-one-frequency-band-out F1 0.437 (> within-band 0.388, >> chance 0.200 -- vowel is predictable even for signs whose FREQUENCY band was never in training) AND deconfounded (frequency-residualized) LOFO F1 0.467. A frequency component exists but is separable (max single-component |corr(comp, log-freq)| 0.429). So the vowel co-occurrence structure is FREQUENCY-INVARIANT LINGUISTIC structure -- NOT the frequency artifact that sank position->C/V / reduced-seed. This CORROBORATES + STRENGTHENS E093 (which showed vowel beats a frequency-only baseline; E099 shows it survives out-of-frequency-distribution generalization + deconfounding). CRITICAL SCOPE: this is BLINDED LINEAR B (which HAS vowel truth); LINEAR A has NO vowel ground truth, so LA invariance is NOT tested -- the epoch exports a confound-adjusted LA embedding to E102 and makes NO LA linguistic-recovery claim. WEAK in absolute terms (F1 ~0.44). L2, opaque signs, no phonetic values, no translations, no licence.

title: Topological data analysis: H0 persistence (persistent connected components) over the sign context-distance filtration; do persistent communities align with linguistic classes vs metric-permutation + frequency-matched nulls; LA inventory-stability.
verdict: PERSISTENT_STRUCTURE_GENERIC (mechanical) -- persistent H0 communities EXIST and are inventory-stable (LA persistent-partition stability across min_count 2 vs 3 inventories ARI 0.729) but do NOT align with linguistic classes on blinded LB: best persistent-partition vowel ARI -0.002 (~0, BELOW the metric-permutation null p95 0.023 AND the frequency-matched null p95 0.024), consonant best ARI 0.019. So the persistent topology recoverable by H0 persistence is GENERIC (degree / single-linkage chaining structure), NOT a distinctively linguistic topology. NOTABLE: the real, frequency-invariant vowel signal established in E099 (F1 ~0.44) does NOT manifest as SEPARATED persistent clusters -- it is a weak centroid-separable signal, not a topological-cluster signal (single-linkage chains through it). LA persistent communities are exported as ANONYMOUS + inventory-stable for E102, with NO semantics assigned. L2, opaque signs, no phonetic values, no licence.

title: Global network-flow constrained decipherment benchmark: capacity-Hungarian global assignment vs local-NN/random; Stage-A known-target blinded LB, Stage-B target-free LA; does global optimization add info beyond the anchor-lattice null?
verdict: GLOBAL_ASSIGNMENT_SELECTS_ARBITRARY_REPRESENTATIVE (mechanical) -- global optimization does NOT reduce LA ambiguity beyond the anchor-lattice null. Stage A (blinded LB, KNOWN target): global assignment barely beats local nearest-neighbor (global 0.485 vs local 0.466, +0.019 -- BELOW the +0.11 margin the PC shows global achieves when it genuinely helps; random 0.167), i.e. even WITH a target the global marginal constraint adds ~nothing on real LB. Stage B (LA, TARGET-FREE): with no cognate target and no external anchors, global assignment produces equivalence CLASSES whose labels are unidentifiable up to the full symmetric group S_5 (120 equivalent relabelings) -- global optimization is strictly a REPRESENTATIVE-SELECTOR; it cannot manufacture the constraints the anchor-lattice showed are absent (~10^63-10^270 degeneracy). This mechanically distinguishes LA from the global-optimization re-decipherment SUCCESSES (Ugaritic etc.), which all had a KNOWN related-language TARGET that LA lacks. NO ambiguity reduction beyond the anchor-lattice null. L2, opaque signs, no phonetic values, no licence.

title: Cross-method synthesis + frozen LA application: dependency-adjusted independence audit of the vowel-class relation across method families + absolute-value gate; frozen LA relative-only outputs.
verdict: SYNTHESIS_NO_INDEPENDENT_CHANNELS (mechanical) -- the honest terminal state of the cross-disciplinary batch. The ONLY above-null structure (vowel-class co-occurrence communities; best ARI 0.176) rests on a SINGLE independent channel (context co-occurrence): eigenmap + ICA + morphogenesis (E091) + Potts (E098) + persistence (E100) ALL share that one lineage (eigenmap|ica agreement 0.979), so under the dependency-adjusted independence audit they collapse to ONE channel, NOT five. The one genuinely INDEPENDENT channel tested (positional profile) does NOT recover vowel (ARI 0.044 ~ chance). Therefore no relation clears the >=2-independent-channel bar, and the absolute-value gate FAILS on every criterion (>=2 channels NO, LOO-anchor NO, held-out-form-value-prediction NO). FROZEN LA APPLICATION is restricted to RELATIVE ANONYMOUS classes + per-sign class-margin (uncertainty) + ranked evidence-acquisition targets -- NO sound values, NO translations, NO language-family ID (all forbidden outputs withheld in code). Net F12: ONE real-but-weak, single-channel, LA-UNTESTABLE linguistic signal (E099 frequency-invariant LB vowel) + a wall of LA-transfer/identify negatives (E097 BP, E098 Potts, E100 TDA, E101 network-flow) + the morphogenesis nulls (E091-E095). Consistent with the anchor-lattice underdetermination. L2, opaque signs, no phonetic values, no licence.

title: Campaign-wide adaptive null E104R: corrected categorical maxT (M=5000, aligned with E103's original prereg commitment), n=400 nulls, bound-based certification criterion committed blind BEFORE any corrected-machinery null ran; naive ablation arm on the SAME 400 nulls.
verdict: CAMPAIGN_NULL_GATES_CERTIFIED — the graduation decision rule (corrected categorical best-of-universe maxT x 3 overlapping segmentation/selection variants x >=2/3 same-top-sign agreement) falsely graduates 1/400 on structure-matched signal-free nulls: one-sided exact CP95 upper bound 1.18% <= 2%. Naive best-of raw-p ablation on the SAME 400 nulls: 121/400 (CP95 upper 34.25%) -> suppression factor 121x; the correlation-aware maxT deflation is the load-bearing gate. Planted 12% prefix recovered 40/40. The ORIGINAL E104 run (1/200, CP95 upper 2.35%) is henceforth characterized as a PILOT: a valid empirical calibration of the rule-as-implemented, not a certification of rate <= 2% at 95% confidence. Scope notes (committed): 'end-to-end' means the graduation DECISION RULE end-to-end (PC and LOMO are not re-executed per null); the absolute-value-gate zero is structural (a positional-shuffle null has a single axis, by construction). E103R's A graduation (z~17, ~2.4x next sign, 3/3 variants) sits far outside this certified null band.
